TTIS Onboarding Solution — Technical Architecture Summary

Document — Onboarding Module (R-OB-01) architecture & 90-day process reference Audience — systems architects, GovCon compliance, engineering Status — as built and merged to main (PRs #83–#94) Companion — Product Design Spec: PDS_ONBOARDING_MODULE.md

This is the authoritative design reference for the Onboarding Module: what it is, how it is built, and the step-by-step 90-day end-to-end process — triggers, gates, agents, and owners.

1. What it is & where it sits

The Onboarding Module turns the TTIS Employee Onboarding Policy v1.0 (§1–§20) into a VBS subsystem: a per-hire record with a phase timeline, a policy-derived task checklist, a versioned acknowledgment ledger, a training roster with transactionally-enforced access gates, screening, asset issuance, milestone check-ins, deadline reminders, and chain-anchored sign-offs.

Layer Artifact
Service / domain logic dashboard/onboarding.py
Program-as-data (the policy) config/onboarding_program.yaml
HTTP API dashboard/main.py → /api/v1/onboarding/* (17 routes)
AuthZ dashboard/abac.py — onboarding resource + CUI keystone
Web UI dashboard/web.py + templates — board / detail / self-view / assistant
Persistence db.py abstraction — SQLite (local) ↔ Cloud SQL Postgres (prod)
Audit orchestration/audit_logger.py — SHA-256 hash chain

Design principles (deliberate)

  • Deterministic, zero-LLM core. Creation, tasks, acks, training, gates, completion, sweep, and exports are pure CRUD + FSM. 0 model calls in any core flow.
  • Domain FSM lives in the database, not the workflow engine. The workflow engine's context is Redis with a 72-hour TTL; a 90-day process needs durable relational state. The module follows the timecard pattern (DB rows + OCC + step-up + strict anchors).
  • Fail-closed evidence. Every governed mutation writes a log_event_strict anchor inside the same DB transaction; if the anchor fails, the transaction rolls back. No signed / released / completed-but-unanchored state can exist.
  • Pointers-only, no PII. No PII column exists in the schema. doc_ref / evidence_ref / result_ref / case_ref are opaque references into the personnel store. Notifications and exports carry IDs + codes only.
  • Stateless / externally triggered. No in-process timers; correct on Cloud Run or compose-on-VM.

2. Data model (the onboarding logical DB)

onboarding_records (FSM + phase + FR-2 flags + completion_hash + OCC version) is the parent of:

onboarding_tasks · onboarding_acknowledgments · onboarding_training · onboarding_access_gates · onboarding_screening · onboarding_assets · onboarding_checkins, plus a reference table compliance_refs (FAR clause citations, counsel-confirmed).

All IDs are module-generated TEXT; timestamps ISO-8601 TEXT; booleans INTEGER 0/1; every mutation carries an OCC version (v1, v2…). DDL is dialect-portable (passes _pg_adapt).

3. Triggers (how onboarding starts and is driven)

Trigger Mechanism Owner / actor
Create (primary) POST /api/v1/onboarding/records — capability onboarding:create (CEO-only today; OQ-2) HR (as CEO) or service principal
Create (auto on hire) HRDB.write(operation=hire\|onboard, data={"onboarding": {...}}) → best-effort hook → create_record(...). Opt-in (no onboarding directive ⇒ no record); never fails the hire NEXUS (HR agent) or HR via the HR tool
Asset-recovery (separation) HRDB.write(terminate\|offboard) → flag_assets_for_recovery() → issued assets → recovery_due + P2 notice HR / NEXUS
Deadline sweep (reminders) POST /api/v1/onboarding/sweep — idempotent, capability onboarding:trigger. No in-process timer — wired to an external Cloud Scheduler job / VM cron (OQ-1/OQ-3) Scheduler / service principal

At creation, the caller supplies FR-2 flags that tailor the program:

is_field · is_time_charging · is_cui_authorized · everify_required · screening_level (none|standard|contract) · labor_standards_covered (none|sca|dba|both) · division.

The service then instantiates only the applicable tasks, courses, gates, and screenings from config. A field + time-charging + CUI hire gets PPE/OSHA tasks, DCAA + CMMC + CUI training, and both access gates; an office hire gets none of those.

4. The 90-day end-to-end process

Record FSM: preboarding → active → completed (+ cancelled from any non-terminal state). Phase pointer: preboarding → day_one → week_1 → day_30 → day_60 → day_90 → complete.

Phase is computed, never client-set: derived from start_date (config offsets: week_1=+7, day_30/60/90=+30/60/90) and held back by any open statutory task — e.g. an unverified I-9 keeps the record at day_one regardless of the calendar. Due dates are stamped at instantiation as offsets from start_date.

Legend — Owner (owner_role): HR, HM = Hiring Manager, IT, SEC = Security Officer, NH = New Hire. ⛔ = statutory (non-waivable). 🔒 = gate-relevant. * = conditional on FR-2 flags.

Phase 0 — Pre-boarding (offer-accept → Day 0)

Item Owner Day Notes
Offer packet & new-hire paperwork sent HR −5
Background screening initiated HR −5 if screening_level≥standard; onboarding_screening row opens
Accounts/equipment provisioning prepared (least-privilege) IT −2 §14
Day-one schedule communicated HM −2

Status preboarding. Screening rows (background, hspd12_piv if contract, everify_case if enabled) start as multi-day external processes. Record auto-activates when start_date arrives.

Phase 1 — Day One (Day 0 → 3)

Item Owner Day Notes
Form I-9 Section 1 NH 0 ⛔ statutory · requires doc_ref · holds the phase
System accounts provisioned (least-privilege) IT 1 §14
PPE issued & fit-checked HM 1 field only
Form I-9 Section 2 verification HR 3 ⛔ statutory · requires doc_ref · holds the phase
E-Verify case created HR 3 only if a FAR E-Verify clause flows down (OQ-4; default off)
Payroll enrollment (W-4, direct deposit) HR 3 §15
MFA enrollment confirmed IT 3 §11

Acknowledgments become due (in-page signable on the self-view): Employee Handbook, Standards of Conduct, IT Acceptable Use, Workplace Safety — plus site-specific safety for field personnel.

Phase 2 — First Week (Day 7)

Item Owner Day Notes
Role expectations & initial goals set HM 7 §5.3
Job-site safety briefing HM 7 field
Wage-determination communicated HR 7 SCA/DBA hires (OQ-6)
HSPD-12 / PIV credentialing initiated SEC 7 contract hires; blocked until the hspd12_piv screening is cleared

Training is assigned with due dates (see §5). DCAA timekeeping is due Day 5 — earliest, because it gates time-charging.

Phase 3 — 30 / 60 / 90-day ramp

Item Owner Day Notes
Benefits enrollment (or written waiver on file) HR 30 §15, evidence pointer
Day-30 check-in HM 30 first-class onboarding_checkins row · strict-anchored
OSHA outreach module due (training) 60 field (OSHA 10/30 per role — OQ-7)
Day-60 check-in HM 60 strict-anchored
Day-90 check-in HM 90 sequence-enforced — requires 30 & 60 first

Access gates are released across this window as their precondition training completes (below).

Phase 4 — Completion sign-off (≈ Day 90)

POST /records/{id}/complete (capability approve + step-up MFA + If-Match) server-verifies the entire program in one query set: no open required tasks · all required acks signed at current version · all required training completed/waived · all gates released · all three check-ins recorded. On pass → fail-closed log_event_strict → completion_hash stored on the record → status completed, phase complete. The completion certificate is the record view + its chain hash, verifiable via verify_chain() and exportable.

Counterpart: POST /records/{id}/cancel — step-up + written reason + strict anchor — terminates a no-show / rescinded offer.

5. Training roster & the two access gates (the compliance teeth)

Courses (config catalog; instantiated by flag):

Course Required for Due (day) Hard-gate?
ethics_conduct, mandatory_disclosure, anti_kickback all (§9) 30 —
dcaa_timekeeping time-charging (§9) 5 🔒 unwaivable
cyber_awareness all (§11) 14 —
cui_handling, cmmc_nist_awareness CUI-authorized (§11) 14 🔒 unwaivable
eeo_anti_harassment all (§12) 30 —
safety_orientation all (§13) 14 —
osha_module field (§13) 60 —
labor_standards_orientation SCA/DBA (§10) 14 —

Gates (onboarding_access_gates, durable rows, held → released). Release = PATCH /gates/{id}/release — OCC match + precondition verification + step-up + strict anchor, all inside one transaction:

Gate Created when Preconditions Enforcement while held
time_charging is_time_charging dcaa_timekeeping completed The hire cannot submit a timecard → 403 onboarding_gate_held. Enforced in-handler at timecard submit (drafts still allowed). Turns §9 into authorization.
cui_provisioning is_cui_authorized cmmc_nist_awareness and cui_handling completed (passing) The hire is held out of CUI-bearing resources for any action → 403. Enforced in require_capability (the universal auth dependency) for resources pipeline, documents, lynne, eaa_ops (OQ-10). Turns §11 "no access until trained" into authorization.

The §9/§11 hard-gate courses can never be waived (server-enforced 422), and a gate cannot be released as a side effect of a training write — two deliberate actions = two audit anchors. CEO is exempt from the keystone (predicate #1 precedes); the lookup is defensive (no record/gate ⇒ no block), so it is a no-op for everyone not mid-onboarding.

6. Owners & roles (accountability model)

Each task carries an owner_role (the accountable human function). The ABAC PDP maps those to enforced capabilities:

owner_role ABAC role (enforced) Onboarding capabilities
hr / it / security CEO (allow-all today — OQ-2: dedicated HR/IT roles deferred) create, update, approve, waive, release, complete, export
hiring_manager FIELD_OPS_MGR view, update, approve — division-scoped (predicate #5)
(benefits / payroll) FINANCE view + export (incl. the auditor view/export-only posture)
new_hire FIELD_CREW view + submit — own record only (server-side ownership predicate); may sign own acks and complete own new_hire tasks in-page
BUSINESS_DEV — no access · auditor flag → mutations denied everywhere

7. Agents (deliberately agent-light)

The onboarding core is deterministic — it dispatches no AI agents. Agent involvement is at the edges:

Agent / surface Role in onboarding
NEXUS (HR agent — A10, Sonnet; tools hr_db_read/write, document_search, notification_send) Its authorized-hire path (HRDB.write hire/onboard) is the auto-create trigger (P4 hook). NEXUS initiates; it does not run the program.
Onboarding assistant (read-only, FIELDAIDE-style, EAA Claude client) New-hire-facing Q&A — get_my_onboarding_status + search_policy, principal-bound (can only read the caller's own record), zero writes. Exposed at POST /api/v1/onboarding/assistant and the /app/onboarding/assistant chat page.
COMMAND-CORE (deterministic event router) Not used for onboarding — the FSM is DB-resident by design; only edge events (creation in, notifications out) touch it.
CIPHER (IT), SENTINEL (Safety), NEXUS as reviewer Exist in the fleet and align to the IT/Safety/HR owner roles, but are not wired into the onboarding FSM. The mark_own_task_complete write tool is the one deferred agent capability (gated on a decision to let an LLM mutate tasks).

8. Cross-cutting controls

  • OCC on every mutation (If-Match → 428 missing / 409 stale).
  • Step-up MFA (X-Step-Up) on gate release, waivers, completion, cancellation (returns 403 step_up_required — the dashboard-wide convention; drives an MFA flow, not a logout).
  • Idempotency on create / ack / sweep (replay-safe).
  • Audit: every sign-off, ack, training result, gate pass, waiver, screening clear, and completion writes a fail-closed log_event_strict whose chain hash is stored on the row; the whole trail is verify_chain()-able.
  • Reminders: the sweep emits P2 (owner-email) for statutory items, P3 otherwise, idempotently (per-item/-tier stamps), with employee_id + code only.
  • Realtime: record_created / gate_released / completed / cancelled publish best-effort on the existing workflows channel.

Verification posture: validated at unit (80+ service tests), HTTP-integration (TestClient driving real auth → ABAC → endpoint → DB → audit chain, incl. the live keystone), and live-server levels (booted against real Redis; board / detail / self-view, the assistant chat, and the in-page sign / complete bridges all confirmed end-to-end).

9. Open questions baked into the timeline

The engine enforces the structure today; the values below drop into config/onboarding_program.yaml and compliance_refs when HR/legal confirm — no code change required:

OQ Item
OQ-1 / OQ-3 Sweep trigger wiring (Cloud Scheduler vs VM cron)
OQ-2 Dedicated HR / IT / Security ABAC roles (mapped to CEO today)
OQ-4 E-Verify applicability (FAR clause / Texas) — default off
OQ-6 SCA / DBA wage determinations; HSPD-12 / PIV contracts
OQ-7 OSHA 10 vs 30 by role; drug/alcohol screening policy
OQ-9 Retention periods (I-9, personnel, training records)
OQ-10 CUI-bearing resource set (wired: pipeline, documents, lynne, eaa_ops)
OQ-12 Passing scores, benefits window, ack document versions

Every component referenced as existing is implemented and merged to main. Bracketed [VERIFY] / [PLACEHOLDER] items require counsel/compliance review and are config-driven, not code.

TTIS AI Virtual Business System — Onboarding Module (R-OB-01). Generated from docs/ONBOARDING_ARCHITECTURE.md; diagram inlined from docs/onboarding_90day_swimlane.svg.
